channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source coding-focused agentic model with Mixture-of-Experts architecture and one trillion parameters. The Kimi Code CLI enables terminal-based repository exploration, file editing, and test execution with plan mode for codebase investigation.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 concludes on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco with keynotes, main-stage programming, and startup showcase. The livestream covers the final day of this industry conference.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 livestream held July 1, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming covering AI engineering topics throughout the day.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 took place on June 30, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco, featuring keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day. The event was streamed online by AI Engineer, covering one of the largest gatherings in AI engineering.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI recommends building an Agentic OS framework to enhance Claude Code capabilities through skills management, loop engineering, and memory management. The approach provides customization benefits and enables sharing outputs with non-technical teams.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.

item.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of software development results while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) accounts for 90%. The video frames agentic engineering as the successor to vibe coding and emphasizes context engineering and orchestration skills as critical for 2026.

item.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created simonw/browser-compat-db, a SQLite database conversion of Mozilla's browser compatibility data. The project includes Claude-generated Python scripts for data transformation, a GitHub Actions workflow built with GPT-4.5, and makes the database publicly accessible via GitHub CDN with CORS support for exploration via Datasette Lite.

item.title: What we learned from 1,604 Chinese AI job postings
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI analyzed 1,604 Chinese AI job postings to infer the research strategies and priorities of Chinese AI labs. The analysis uses hiring patterns as a window into organizational focus areas and technology direction.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The company brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the traditional IT services sector.

item.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a team-based version of Claude Code that operates within Slack, integrating with company tools and enabling task management in shared channels. This represents a shift in AI deployment from private chat interfaces to collaborative enterprise workflows.

item.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
item.description: chatmasta: Omnigent is an open-source framework that provides a meta harness for agents. It appears designed to standardize or simplify the development and deployment of AI agent systems.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as a public company, causing its stock price to decline. The CEO attributed the market reaction to a misunderstanding of the company's margin outlook.

item.title: A24 Knows You're Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: nate: A24 is facing backlash from its audience over a collaboration with Google AI. The announcement has generated negative public sentiment regarding the entertainment company's partnership with the tech giant.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports on SignalFire data showing that engineering jobs are resilient despite predictions of AI-driven displacement. Engineers represent a larger share of new hires in AI-affected sectors, contradicting narratives about widespread job elimination.

item.title: AI Data Center Power Requirements in 2026: The Complete Grid-to-Chip Guide
item.description: lopespm: lopespm presents an analysis of projected AI data center power consumption through 2026, covering infrastructure scaling from grid to chip-level systems. The piece examines power requirements as AI workloads expand.

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are departing Google to join Anthropic, continuing a trend of high-profile scientist exits from Google that previously included Noam Shazeer and John Jumper. This reflects competitive pressure in hiring and retaining AI talent.

item.title: OpenAI Codex bombards SSDs with needless write operations
item.description: jgalt212: OpenAI Codex generates excessive write operations to SSDs, potentially damaging storage hardware through unnecessary disk activity. The report highlights inefficient I/O behavior in the model's execution pipeline.

item.title: How to Automate Content Creation With AI in 2026 (AI Content Repurposing)
item.description: AI Master: Trupeer AI automates the video editing and content repurposing workflow by converting screen recordings into multiple polished outputs including tutorial videos, SOP documents, and multi-language translations in minutes rather than hours. The platform offers custom AI avatar creation, voice cloning, and one-click translation features to streamline content production.

item.title: Anthropic says Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude AI model capabilities
item.description: htrp: Anthropic publicly alleges that Alibaba has illicitly extracted capabilities from Claude AI model. This is a claim about unauthorized model distillation or extraction of proprietary model behavior.

item.title: Claude 5.1 Confirmed: What Happens After The Fable 5 Ban?
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video claims Claude 5 (codenamed Fable) was banned by the U.S. government 76 hours after launch following superior performance on benchmarks and capability throttling. The video contextualizes the fictional ban within the competitive AI race and discusses geopolitical leverage theories around OpenAI and other labs.

item.title: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
item.description: Latent Space: Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin from Databricks argue that the frontier AI ecosystem must be open for companies to build agent clouds at scale. The discussion frames openness as a prerequisite for broader adoption of AI agent infrastructure.

item.title: The Patron Saint of Murderers - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on a YouTube podcast. The specific content details are not provided in the source material.

item.title: Quoting Tom MacWright
item.description: Simon Willison: Tom MacWright observes that job applicants are increasingly submitting portfolios wholly generated by LLMs, from resume text to portfolio sites to GitHub commits. He argues that LLM-generated materials are generic and reveal nothing about the candidate except tool familiarity, creating "accidental anonymity" that obscures the person behind the application.

item.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
item.description: jamdesk: OpenAI and Broadcom have unveiled Jalapeno, OpenAI's first custom inference chip. The chip is designed for AI model inference optimization and represents OpenAI's move into proprietary hardware development.

item.title: Computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
item.description: swolpers: Google's Gemini 3.5 Flash model now supports computer use, enabling the AI to interact with and control computer systems. This is a new capability addition to the Gemini product line.

item.title: Thinking to recall: How reasoning unlocks parametric knowledge in LLMs
item.description: Google Research: Research from Google examines how reasoning processes enable LLMs to better access and utilize parametric knowledge embedded in their weights. The work demonstrates that explicit reasoning can unlock information that may otherwise remain inaccessible during standard inference.

item.title: “I spent $50,000 self-hosting AI models. You should too.” - 0xSero
item.description: David Ondrej: The video presents an argument that self-hosting AI models is worthwhile despite requiring significant capital investment. It covers local AI deployment, various models like GLM 5.2 and Fable 5, and discusses practical considerations for running inference infrastructure independently.

item.title: Big AI labs are hiring philosophers
item.description: Brajeshwar: Big AI labs are recruiting philosophers to join their teams. This reflects growing recognition that philosophical expertise is relevant to AI development and deployment.

item.title: Introducing computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
item.description: Google DeepMind: Google DeepMind announced computer use capability in Gemini 3.5 Flash, enabling the model to interact with computer interfaces and perform tasks autonomously.

item.title: Accelerating BEV Pooling on NVIDIA GPUs for Physical AI Applications
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: The article discusses accelerating bird's-eye-view pooling on NVIDIA GPUs for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and spatial AI systems. BEV perception is presented as a common design pattern that projects data into a top-down view representation.

item.title: How Model Context Protocol (MCP) actually works
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol that enables AI agents to discover and interact with external tools and data without custom integration code. Google Cloud explains how MCP differs from traditional APIs by supporting probabilistic AI reasoning and standardizing agent-to-resource connections through a client-server architecture with tools, prompts, resources, and context components.

item.title: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel
item.description: Hugging Face: NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel is a framework designed to accelerate transformer fine-tuning. The post from Hugging Face describes how this tool enables faster and more efficient transformer training.

item.title: Are ChatGPT and other AI chatbots politically biased? We tested them. - The Washington Post
item.description: Google News AI: The Washington Post conducted tests to examine whether ChatGPT and other AI chatbots exhibit political bias. The investigation evaluates how major conversational AI systems respond to politically sensitive queries and topics.

item.title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, discusses how product management is evolving as code becomes abundant while market opportunities remain scarce. She argues that constraints have shifted from building capability to finding market fit, and that modern PMs must focus on discovering novel ideas and validating demand rather than managing engineering capacity.

item.title: What Is Your Job Now, Farhan Thawar | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Farhan Thawar, head of engineering at Shopify, discusses how an engineer's job transforms when AI writes most code. Topics include shifts in the software development lifecycle, learning as collateral damage, moving bottlenecks, and the boundary between prototyping and production systems.

item.title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, argues that database infrastructure must be redesigned for agent-driven workflows. The talk covers agent-optimized database operations including sharding, branching, deploys, safety mechanisms, and the importance of human-first infrastructure design to enable safe autonomous optimization loops.

item.title: For most of the world, open-source AI is the only way forward
item.description: CrankyBear: The post argues that open-source AI models are essential for global access to AI technology. The author contends that proprietary models create barriers that exclude most of the world from meaningful AI adoption.

item.title: Variational Autoencoder Layer
item.description: Gananath R: The paper proposes using Variational Autoencoders as neural network layers rather than standalone models. It introduces a new training strategy for models incorporating VAE layers and provides performance analysis. This approach leverages VAEs' probabilistic properties and smooth latent space generation for integration into larger architectures.

item.title: RubyLLM: A Ruby framework for all major AI providers
item.description: doener: RubyLLM is a Ruby framework that provides abstraction and integration with major AI providers. It enables Ruby developers to work with multiple LLM services through a common interface.

item.title: Enhancing Brain MRI Anomaly Detection and Reasoning with ROI Rethink and Synthetic Data
item.description: Shangkun Li, Jie Xu, Yi Guo: BrReMark introduces explicit bounding box marking and hypothesis-verification reasoning into brain MRI diagnosis. The framework combines supervised fine-tuning on reasoning trajectories with reinforcement learning optimizing localization and diagnostic accuracy, plus domain randomization-based pathology synthesis for robustness to out-of-distribution data.

item.title: A 3D-Printable Dataset for Fair Testing and Comparisons of Tactile Sensors
item.description: Dexter R. Shepherd, Nicolas Herzig, Phil Husbands: A 3D-printable dataset of mathematically defined textures designed for fair comparison of tactile sensors across different hardware. The dataset uses parametrically generated surface patterns from sine-wave and Fourier functions with controlled spatial frequency and amplitude. Reproducibility testing across printers and filament types identifies print quality factors affecting sensor measurements.

item.title: An Analysis of Posterior Collapse, Parameterization and Initialization in Variational Deep Gaussian Processes
item.description: Francisco Javier Sáez-Maldonado, Juan Maroñas, Daniel Hernández-Lobato: The paper analyzes posterior collapse in variational deep Gaussian processes (DGPs), showing it connects to the DSVI algorithm and linear prior mean functions. The authors propose an alternative initialization for zero prior mean DGPs that prevents posterior collapse, improves optimization conditioning, and achieves comparable performance without imposing optimization-driven constraints on the prior.

item.title: USS: Unified Spatial-Semantic Prompts for Embodied Visual Tracking with Latent Dynamics Learning
item.description: Yuchen Xie, Xinyu Zhou, Kuangji Zuo: USS introduces unified spatial-semantic prompting for embodied visual tracking, supporting text, point, bounding box, and mask prompts within a single architecture. The method incorporates a latent world model for temporal robustness and demonstrates that explicit spatial cues outperform text-only prompts in complex tracking scenarios.
